column4: Synergistic inhibition of platelet aggregation
column5: Significantly increased bleeding risk
column6: Avoid combination or monitor INR closely; use lowest effective aspirin dose

column4: NSAID inhibits platelet function and displaces warfarin from protein binding
column5: Major bleeding risk including GI haemorrhage
column6: Avoid NSAIDs; use paracetamol for pain relief instead; monitor INR if unavoidable

column4: Rifampicin strongly induces CYP2C9 and CYP3A4
column5: Markedly reduced anticoagulant effect leading to thrombosis risk
column6: Avoid combination; if unavoidable increase warfarin dose significantly and monitor INR daily

column4: Fluconazole potently inhibits CYP2C9 reducing warfarin metabolism
column5: Markedly elevated INR and major bleeding risk
column6: Avoid fluconazole in warfarin patients; if needed reduce warfarin dose by 50% and monitor INR daily

column4: IV contrast can impair renal function causing metformin accumulation
column5: Risk of lactic acidosis particularly with pre-existing renal impairment
column6: Hold metformin 48h before contrast; restart only when renal function confirmed normal

column4: Additive vasodilation via cGMP and cAMP pathways
column5: Severe hypotension cardiovascular collapse and death
column6: Absolutely contraindicated; do not administer within 24h (48h for tadalafil)

column4: Additive vasodilation via cGMP and cAMP pathways
column5: Severe hypotension cardiovascular collapse and death
column6: Absolutely contraindicated; do not administer within 48h of tadalafil

column4: Serotonin accumulation due to dual inhibition of reuptake and metabolism
column5: Potentially fatal serotonin syndrome with hyperthermia rigidity and rhabdomyolysis
column6: Contraindicated; wait at least 14 days after stopping MAOI before starting SSRI

column4: Additive serotonergic effect and lowered seizure threshold
column5: Serotonin syndrome and seizure risk
column6: Avoid combination; if necessary monitor closely for tremor agitation and hyperthermia

column4: Additive serotonergic and noradrenergic effect
column5: Severe serotonin syndrome hyperpyrexia and cardiovascular instability
column6: Absolutely contraindicated

column4: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein reducing digoxin clearance
column5: Digoxin toxicity with bradycardia arrhythmia visual disturbances and nausea
column6: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone; monitor digoxin levels closely

column4: Combined potassium retention via dual renin-angiotensin-aldosterone blockade
column5: Life-threatening hyperkalemia causing cardiac arrest
column6: Avoid or monitor serum potassium weekly; reduce spironolactone dose

column4: Divalent cations chelate fluoroquinolone in GI tract
column5: Reduced ciprofloxacin absorption by up to 90%
column6: Administer ciprofloxacin 2h before or 6h after antacids/dairy/iron

column4: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 causing statin accumulation
column5: Rhabdomyolysis with myopathy and acute renal failure
column6: Switch to pravastatin or rosuvastatin; do not exceed simvastatin 10mg with CYP3A4 inhibitors

column4: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4 causing statin accumulation
column5: Rhabdomyolysis with myopathy and acute renal failure
column6: Temporarily withhold statin or use lowest dose during clarithromycin course

column4: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate
column5: Methotrexate toxicity with mucositis cytopenias and hepatotoxicity
column6: Avoid NSAIDs within 24h of methotrexate; use paracetamol if analgesic needed

column4: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate
column5: Methotrexate toxicity with mucositis cytopenias and hepatotoxicity
column6: Avoid NSAIDs within 24h of methotrexate; use paracetamol if analgesic needed

column4: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 reducing ethinylestradiol and progestin levels
column5: Contraceptive failure and unintended pregnancy
column6: Use additional non-hormonal contraception; consider alternative anticonvulsant

column4: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis impairing lithium excretion
column5: Lithium toxicity with tremor nausea polyuria confusion and cardiac arrhythmia
column6: Avoid NSAIDs; use paracetamol; monitor lithium levels if NSAID unavoidable

column4: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 reducing conversion of clopidogrel to active metabolite
column5: Reduced antiplatelet effect increasing cardiovascular event risk
column6: Switch to pantoprazole or rabeprazole which have less CYP2C19 inhibition

column4: Additive CNS and respiratory depression
column5: Severe respiratory depression apnea and death
column6: Avoid combination; if opioid needed use lowest dose and monitor closely

column4: Additive CNS and respiratory depression
column5: Enhanced sedation respiratory depression and risk of overdose
column6: Avoid alcohol during codeine therapy; counsel patients explicitly

column4: Ciprofloxacin strongly inhibits CYP1A2 causing massive tizanidine accumulation
column5: Severe hypotension bradycardia and excessive sedation
column6: Absolutely contraindicated; use alternative antibiotic

column4: Paroxetine strongly inhibits CYP2D6 blocking conversion of tamoxifen to active endoxifen
column5: Reduced tamoxifen efficacy increasing breast cancer recurrence risk
column6: Avoid paroxetine and fluoxetine in tamoxifen patients; use escitalopram or venlafaxine instead

column4: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-dependent renal vasodilation and blunt ACE inhibitor effect
column5: Acute kidney injury and reduced antihypertensive effect
column6: Avoid NSAIDs; if unavoidable monitor renal function and blood pressure closely

column4: Omeprazole modestly inhibits CYP2C19 increasing phenytoin levels
column5: Mild phenytoin toxicity risk with nystagmus and ataxia at high levels
column6: Monitor phenytoin levels periodically; interaction usually manageable
